note: Canonical source / scope / review-event registry. One source has one record; one reading has one review event; every method, paper, notice, graph edge and UI statement resolves these objects instead of embedding a private copy. Surfaces are described by surfaceType and accessExtent, not ranked on an ordinal depth ladder. The two PMC author manuscripts carry objective byte pins independently fetched by Codex; recording bytes is not a scientific review. No independent (second-reader) event exists yet.

reviewers.name: Claude Code round-4 primary-source pass (implementer, not an independent reviewer)
Round-1 ingestion pass (crossref routes re-queried in the 2026-07-23 Codex review)
Claude Code round-8 content-migration pass (implementer, not an independent reviewer)

surfaceTypeReference: surfaceType is the document surface that was opened; accessExtent is how much of it. A legacy verificationDepth is derived from the surface for backward-compatible counts but is not the statement shown to a reader.

reviewEvents.boundary: Read in full on 2026-07-24. The notice changes one sentence of the Acknowledgements, adding "and University of Texas PDX Development and Trial Center grant U54CA224065" to the list of sources supporting PDX generation and annotation, and states that the original Article has been corrected online. It touches funding attribution only; no method, figure, dataset or conclusion is mentioned.

Read in full on 2026-07-24. Verbatim: due to a processing error, text from a reference title was inserted into the seventh sentence of the Discussion, which now reads "In conclusion, ferroptosis sensitivity explains the higher susceptibility of male individuals to AKI" in the HTML and PDF. Nothing else is corrected and the notice makes no statement about the article's conclusions, so none is recorded here.

Read in full on 2026-07-24. The notice states verbatim that "None of these mistakes affects any scientific conclusions" and that the errors arose in typesetting and were not present in the peer-reviewed version or the source data. What changed: in Fig. 2e a 4OH-E2 structure appeared instead of 17-beta-estradiol and in Fig. 2f the 2OH-E2 structure carried "H" instead of "HO"; in Fig. 2o,p the Sham and IRI OVX labels now reading n = 6 originally read n = 2 and n = 5, and in Fig. 2q the Sham OVX group now reading n = 5 originally read n = 2; several Extended Data cross-references on pages 1011, 1012 and 1014 were wrong; the supplied Supplementary Fig. S3 duplicated plots from Extended Data Fig. 3 and has been replaced; the male and female labels in Extended Data Fig. 2b,c were swapped and the Extended Data Fig. 2c y-axis read "tubular damage score cortex" instead of medulla; and some representative flow cytometry panels in Extended Data Fig. 4d,e were incorrect, with the notice stating the quantified single data points and primary data were correct throughout.

Read in full on 2026-07-24. The notice corrects the catalogue information for one antibody in the Methods section headed "immunoblot analysis": the POR antibody recorded as Abcam ab133303 (clone UOTR1B493, lot GR290350-7) should be Abcam ab180597, 0.076 mg/mL, rabbit mAb to cytochrome P450 reductase, clone EPR 14479(B), lot GR148691-9, used at 1:1,000. It closes with "The errors have been corrected" and makes no statement about any figure, result or conclusion, so nothing about the paper's conclusions is either confirmed or withdrawn by it. Because the correction is confined to reagent identity, it changes which antibody a reader must order to reproduce the POR immunoblots in Fig. 2c, Fig. 3c and Fig. 3d.
Read as the PMC author manuscript, not the paywalled version of record; rendered figure panels and the supplement were not opened. One study's implementation, not a universal protocol.

The abstract states that GPX4 p.R152H disrupts membrane anchoring without considerably impairing catalytic activity, that spatiotemporal Gpx4 deletion or neuron-specific GPX4-R152H expression degenerates cortical and cerebellar neurons with progressive neuroinflammation, and that patient iPSC-derived cortical neurons and forebrain organoids showed increased ferroptotic vulnerability and were sensitive to ferroptosis inhibition.
The catalogue page gives the probe's chemistry, spectra, storage and reconstitution, but no assay design: it names no specimen, perturbation, control, timing or quantification.
Read as the PMC author manuscript, not the paywalled version of record; rendered figure panels and the supplement were not opened. One study's implementation, not a universal protocol.
